title: Planchonella xylocarpa
uses: Planchonella xylocarpa is a species of plant that has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Planchonella xylocarpa belongs to the family Annonaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Planchonella. The genus Planchonella is part of the larger family Annonaceae, which includes over 200 species of trees and shrubs. Planchonella xylocarpa is native to New Caledonia and is a member of the Southern Hemisphere's tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Planchonella xylocarpa is a part of the broader taxonomic hierarchy of Angiosperm Phylogeny Group III (APG III).
